content: Just as linguistic 'reflexion' as a concept encodes its dual meaning of both physical light reflection and deliberate critical introspection in human vocabulary learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to explicitly retain both these dual functions: 'reflecting' (mirroring) inconsistent problem-solving perspectives, and 'reflexion' (deliberate critical review) of those mirrored outputs to filter out flawed reasoning. This dual encoding creates a more robust self-correction pipeline than current single-function self-reflection frameworks.
